sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: NASA, GE Test Hybrid Engine for Next Decade of Flight
feed.summary: NASA and GE Aerospace have completed initial ground testing of a hybrid-electric engine demonstrator designed for single-aisle aircraft, achieving a significant milestone in NASA's Hybrid Thermally Efficient Core (HyTEC) project. This engine, a modified GE Passport, aims to deliver 10% fuel savings by strategically utilizing electric power to assist the gas turbine. The successful test advances the technology towards potential commercial aviation integration by the 2030s, underscoring GE's broader hybrid-electric ambitions, including its RISE project targeting 20% fuel savings and applications in military and advanced air mobility.

feed.title: Truist to pay $0.52 per share to common stockholders on March 2, 2026
feed.summary: Truist Financial Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.52 per common share, payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 13, 2026. The company also declared cash dividends on several series of preferred stock with varying record and payment dates. Truist reported total assets of $548 billion as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: Truist declares common and preferred stock dividends
feed.summary: Truist Financial Corporation announced that its Board of Directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.52 per common share. The dividend is payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 13, 2026. Additionally, the Board declared regular cash dividends on several series of preferred stock, with various payment and record dates in February and March 2026.

feed.title: Another record year for NextEra Energy
feed.summary: NextEra Energy reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025, with increased earnings per share driven by the expansion of its regulated utility businesses and long-term contracted energy infrastructure. The company exceeded its expectations for 2025 and reaffirmed its long-term adjusted earnings per share growth outlook of more than 8% through 2035, citing a balanced growth strategy and investments in new energy infrastructure. CEO John Ketchum highlighted the company's strong position to meet rising electricity demand in the U.S.

feed.title: Key prediction market putting strong odds on BlackRock's Rieder as next Fed chair
feed.summary: Prediction market Kalshi indicates Rick Rieder, BlackRock's chief bond investment manager, is the leading candidate to succeed Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve Chair, with a 48% probability. This surge in odds for Rieder comes amidst President Trump's preferences for a Fed leader willing to pursue interest rate cuts, aligning with Rieder's perceived dovish stance. The article highlights concerns about the Fed's independence under a new chair influenced by political pressure.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern Corporation (NYSE: NSC) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.35 per share on its common stock, payable on February 20, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 6, 2026. This marks the 174th consecutive quarter the company has distributed a dividend since its founding in 1982. Norfolk Southern operates a 22-state freight transportation network, contributing to the U.S. economy and sustainability efforts.

feed.title: Leidos’ $2.4B ENTRUST deal centers on U.S. utility upgrades
feed.summary: Neuberger Berman Capital Solutions has exited its minority investment in ENTRUST Solutions Group through a sale to Leidos for approximately $2.4 billion. ENTRUST provides consulting, design, and engineering services for U.S. utility and infrastructure customers and has over 3,100 professionals. The transaction is subject to regulatory approvals and is expected to close in Q2 2026.

feed.title: Silver ETF Volume On Par With SPY ETF
feed.summary: The article reports that the trading volume for Silver ETFs has reached parity with the SPY ETF. This indicates a significant increase in interest and investment activity in silver, potentially signaling a notable shift in market focus towards the precious metal. The article provides this information within a broader context of commodity prices and mining news.

feed.title: Abbott: Study shows neuromod systems greatly reduce pain-related healthcare visits
feed.summary: New five-year data from Abbott indicates that its neuromodulation systems, including BurstDR spinal cord stimulation and dorsal root ganglion therapy, significantly reduce pain-related healthcare visits for chronic pain sufferers. The study found an average reduction of 75% and 73% respectively, highlighting the systems' effectiveness in improving patients' quality of life and reducing healthcare burdens. Additionally, Abbott announced FDA approval for prone MRI compatibility with its chronic pain systems, expanding accessibility for patients.

feed.title: lululemon Stock Looks Cheap: Buy Now or Wait for Better Entry Point?
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica (LULU) has seen a significant stock decline over the past year due to soft U.S. demand, promotional pressures, and higher costs, despite growth in international markets. The stock appears cheap with a P/E multiple of 15.1X compared to the industry average of 16.28X, and a P/S ratio of 1.95X below the industry average of 2.35X. While recent holiday season performance and international expansion show positive signs, uncertainties in North American recovery and margin normalization suggest a neutral "Hold" stance for investors.

feed.title: Caterpillar is Set to Report Q4 Earnings: Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in its fourth-quarter 2025 bottom line, despite projected higher revenues. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for earnings per share is $4.67, with revenues estimated at $17.9 billion, representing a 10.4% year-over-year growth. While strong long-term fundamentals support retaining CAT shares, new investors might consider waiting for a better entry point due to its premium valuation and anticipated earnings decline.

feed.title: The Analyst Verdict: Pfizer In The Eyes Of 7 Experts
feed.summary: Seven analysts have provided ratings for Pfizer (NYSE: PFE) over the last quarter, resulting in an average price target of $28.00, down 4.11% from previous estimates. The ratings are primarily "Indifferent" or "Neutral," with one "Buy" and several "Equal-Weight" ratings, indicating a cautious yet not overly bearish sentiment. Despite a decline in revenue growth, Pfizer boasts a high market capitalization, strong net margin (21.26%), and impressive return on equity (3.9%), while facing challenges in return on assets.

feed.title: What's Driving the Market Sentiment Around Medtronic PLC?
feed.summary: Medtronic PLC (NYSE: MDT) has seen a 4.24% decrease in its short interest as a percent of float since its last report, indicating a potentially more bullish sentiment among investors. With 14.40 million shares sold short, representing 1.13% of available shares, it would take traders 2.64 days to cover their positions on average. Compared to its peers, Medtronic PLC has less short interest, suggesting a more favorable market outlook for the company.

feed.title: Humana Stock Plunges After Medicare Rate Shock
feed.summary: Humana's stock plummeted over 20% after the Trump administration proposed a near-zero (0.09%) increase in Medicare Advantage payment rates for 2027, far below the expected 4-6%. This unexpected regulatory change significantly impacted Humana and other major health insurers like UnitedHealth Group, CVS Health, and Elevance Health, triggering a sector-wide selloff due to concerns over tighter margins and regulatory uncertainty. Despite the market reaction, Humana's underlying financial health is noted as robust, with strong revenue growth and healthy valuation metrics, though the regulatory shift introduces significant risk.

feed.title: Expert Outlook: CVS Health Through The Eyes Of 17 Analysts
feed.summary: Seventeen analysts have provided ratings for CVS Health (NYSE: CVS), with an average 12-month price target of $95.18, reflecting a 5.76% increase from the previous average. While the company demonstrates dominant market capitalization and positive revenue growth of 7.8% as of September 30, 2025, it faces challenges with a negative net margin, ROE, and ROA, along with a high debt-to-equity ratio of 1.12. These financial indicators suggest potential hurdles in profitability, asset utilization, and debt management for CVS Health.

feed.title: Pacific Gas & Electric Target of Unusually Large Options Trading (NYSE:PCG)
feed.summary: Pacific Gas & Electric (NYSE:PCG) experienced unusually high call options trading, with investors purchasing 52,975 call options, a 79% increase over typical volume, indicating heightened bullish sentiment. The company's stock traded around $15.11, recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.05, and analysts have raised price targets, though the consensus remains a "Hold." Notably, a director, John O. Larsen, significantly increased his personal stake in the company by purchasing 7,500 shares.

feed.title: Keysight Launches Wireless Coexistence Test Solution to Accelerate Compliance and Innovation
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies has released a new Wireless Coexistence Test Solution designed to automate compliance and accelerate innovation for wireless devices. This platform helps engineers validate device performance in crowded RF environments, particularly vital for regulated markets like medical devices, by aligning with ANSI C63.27 standards. The solution aims to reduce manual setup time, improve repeatability, and identify interference risks earlier in the development cycle.

feed.title: Amgen R&D EVP James Bradner On Upcoming Readouts And Doing Deals Around New Capabilities
feed.summary: Amgen's R&D EVP, James Bradner, discussed upcoming clinical trial readouts and the company's strategy for new capabilities through deals like Dark Blue and DISCO. Amgen anticipates Phase III data in 2026 across cancer and other diseases. The article highlights Amgen's recent acquisitions and pipeline developments, including MariTide, Horizon, and Imdelltra.

feed.title: PepsiCo Leans on Pricing Power: Is Volume Decline a Growing Risk?
feed.summary: PepsiCo has increasingly relied on pricing to drive revenue growth, offsetting rising input costs and protecting margins, especially in North America. While this strategy has supported profitability, it raises concerns about potential volume declines as consumers become more price-sensitive. Competitors like Coca-Cola and Keurig Dr Pepper also employ similar pricing strategies, highlighting a broader industry trend where balancing price realization with affordability is crucial for long-term volume stability.

feed.title: What Should You Expect From Las Vegas Sands' Q4 Earnings?
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands Corp. (LVS) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on January 28, 2026, with an expected EPS increase of 44.4% and revenue growth of 14.9%. The company's performance is anticipated to be driven by strong visitation and mass gaming revenue, particularly in Singapore's Marina Bay Sands and Macao's recovering market. Despite potential margin pressures in Macao due to increased reinvestment and operating costs, Zacks' model predicts an earnings beat for LVS.

feed.title: Popular grocery chain dubbed ‘cheaper than Dollar Tree’ to open another new store in March
feed.summary: H-E-B's spin-off, Joe V’s Smart Shop, is set to open a new store in Garland, Texas, in March, marking its fourth location in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. The grocery chain is popular for its low prices, with some shoppers claiming it's cheaper than Dollar Tree. Construction on the new store is expected to begin on March 1 and finish by July 1, costing an estimated $400,000.

feed.title: Wells FiNet Draws Teams With $1.3-Bln Combined From Ameriprise, Raymond James, UBS
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's independent broker-dealer, FiNet, has recruited three teams with a combined $1.3 billion in assets this month from Ameriprise Financial, Raymond James, and UBS Wealth Management USA. The largest team, Houser, Plessl & Staples Wealth Management Group, managed $530 million at Ameriprise. Other recruits include advisor Paolo Thompson from Raymond James and a team comprising Brandon M. Larson and Todd D. Hanneman from UBS, each bringing substantial assets to FiNet.

feed.title: KLA's Q2 Earnings Loom: Should the KLAC Stock Be in Your Portfolio?
feed.summary: KLA (KLAC) is set to report its Q2 fiscal 2026 results on January 29, with Zacks estimating revenues of $3.26 billion and non-GAAP earnings of $8.82 per share, driven by strong AI infrastructure investment, advanced packaging, and process control demand. Despite a premium valuation compared to some peers, its strong market position, growth prospects, and a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) with a Growth Score of B suggest it could be a valuable addition to an investment portfolio. The company's shares have significantly outperformed its sector and peers over the last 12 months.

feed.title: Corning, Meta agree on $6 billion deal to support data center buildout
feed.summary: Corning Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. have announced a multiyear deal worth up to $6 billion to accelerate the buildout of advanced data centers in the U.S., supporting Meta's AI ambitions. Corning will expand its optical cable manufacturing facility in Hickory, North Carolina, and increase its state employment by 15-20% to fulfill this agreement. This partnership signifies a commitment to strengthening domestic supply chains and manufacturing for critical AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Nisun International Enterprise Development Group (NASDAQ:NISN) sheds 14% this week, as yearly returns fall more in line with earnings growth
feed.summary: Shares of Nisun International Enterprise Development Group (NASDAQ:NISN) dropped 14% this week, though the stock has seen a significant 899% increase over the past three years. This recent dip aligns its yearly returns more closely with its earnings per share growth, which has been 26% annually over the same period, indicating increased market optimism. The company's CEO compensation is below average, and investors are advised to consider other factors beyond just share price performance.

feed.title: Partnership Will Use AI, Autonomous Systems, To Help Fight Catastrophic Fires
feed.summary: PG&E, Lockheed Martin, Salesforce, and Wells Fargo have formed Emberpoint, LLC, a new venture to combat catastrophic wildfires. This collaboration will leverage artificial intelligence, autonomous systems, and integrated command and control technologies to enhance early fire detection, prevention, and mitigation efforts for first responders. The goal is to provide firefighters with faster information and improve safety, with demonstrations of the technology expected later this year.

feed.title: Former Fifth Third C-suite officer joins one of Cincinnati's largest law firms
feed.summary: Susan Zaunbrecher, former Chief Legal Officer of Fifth Third Bancorp, has joined Dinsmore, one of Cincinnati's largest law firms, as director of special projects and strategic initiatives. This move marks a return for Zaunbrecher, who previously spent nearly three decades at Dinsmore before her tenure at Fifth Third. Her appointment comes after her retirement from Fifth Third in July 2025.

feed.title: News | Blackstone sells shopping center in Chicago suburbs at massive discount
feed.summary: Blackstone has sold the Streets of Woodfield shopping center in Schaumburg, Illinois, for $69 million to Hutensky Capital Partners, representing a significant loss from its 2015 purchase price of $168.5 million. This discounted sale highlights challenges in the retail property market, including the impact of COVID-19, retailer bankruptcies, and changing consumer habits. Hutensky Capital plans to invest further in the property, seeing it as an opportunity to build on an already strong tenant lineup and fill vacant spaces.

feed.title: Former MD sues Citigroup alleging biased HR probes and entrenched sexism
feed.summary: A former managing director, Julia Carreon, has filed a lawsuit against Citigroup, alleging race and sex discrimination, a hostile work environment, and constructive discharge. Carreon claims that Citigroup's HR department protected male leaders while targeting women through biased investigations, including one probing whether her promotion was due to an inappropriate relationship with a senior executive. The lawsuit seeks back pay, compensatory and punitive damages, and other relief.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: PACCAR Q4 2025 misses EPS but beats revenue
feed.summary: PACCAR Inc reported mixed Q4 2025 financial results, missing EPS forecasts slightly at $1.06 vs. $1.07 expected, but significantly beating revenue expectations with $6.82 billion against a $6.03 billion forecast. Despite a post-earnings stock dip, the company emphasized robust revenue growth, record performance in PACCAR Parts and Financial Services, and optimism for 2026 due to new product innovations and market clarity on tariffs and emissions. Executives highlighted strategic advantages from local manufacturing and anticipated sequential growth throughout the year.

feed.title: IsoEnergy closes C$25 million private placement with NexGen Energy
feed.summary: IsoEnergy Ltd. has completed a C$25 million private placement with NexGen Energy Ltd., issuing 1,666,667 common shares at C$15.00 each. This transaction allows NexGen to maintain its approximately 30% ownership in IsoEnergy and will fund the development and exploration of IsoEnergy's mineral properties. The company, which trades near its 52-week high, has seen significant returns and is also undertaking a C$50 million bought deal financing and a 2026 winter exploration program at its Larocque East project.

feed.title: Wells Fargo approves $0.45 quarterly dividend payable in March
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE:WFC) has announced that its board of directors approved a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.45 per share, payable on March 1, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 6, 2026. This move follows four consecutive years of dividend increases, with a 12.5% growth in the last twelve months. The announcement comes after the company reported strong Q4 2025 earnings per share, despite slightly missing revenue projections, leading to mixed analyst adjustments of price targets.

feed.title: Apple plans to avoid iPhone price hikes due to memory dearth, but gross margins might take hit: TF
feed.summary: Apple is expected to refrain from raising iPhone prices despite a memory supply shortage spurred by data center demand, which is causing price increases. This strategy, however, is anticipated to negatively impact the company's iPhone gross margins in the near term. The decision aims to keep the upcoming iPhone models accessible to consumers.

feed.title: Caesars Online Casino Awards its Biggest Jackpot Ever in New Jersey
feed.summary: A New Jersey player recently won the largest-ever jackpot across Caesars Entertainment’s online casino platforms, taking home $1.2 million while playing MegaJackpots Cash Eruption. This win marks a new record for online gambling at the company and continues a history of substantial payouts from the specific game. Caesars Digital and IGT spokespeople highlight the game's popularity and ability to create memorable player experiences, while also reminding players to gamble responsibly.

feed.title: State of the Pipeline – January 2026
feed.summary: As of January 21, 2026, two mutual bank conversions are in the pipeline. URSB Bancorp Inc. has filed a prospectus for a mutual-to-stock conversion, with its offering expiring on February 12, focusing on purchased C&I and consumer loans. PSB Financial Inc. also filed for a mutual-to-stock conversion on September 23, 2025, proposing to become the holding company for Pioneer State Bank and considering branch expansion in western Montana.

feed.title: JP Morgan Downgrades ONEOK (OKE), Lowers Price Target to $83 | OKE Stock News Gur
feed.summary: JP Morgan has downgraded ONEOK (OKE) from 'Overweight' to 'Neutral' and reduced its price target to $83 from $87. This adjustment reflects a shift in analyst perspective, following similar changes from other financial institutions. Despite the downgrade, the average target price from 20 analysts is $87.86, suggesting a potential upside from the current price, and GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $102.40.

feed.title: Has Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) Run Too Far After Recent Supply Chain Focus?
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) has seen significant stock price increases recently, driven by its crucial role in global agricultural supply chains. However, two valuation approaches suggest the stock may be overvalued: a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis implies a 128.8% overvaluation, and its P/E ratio of 27.23x is higher than its customized Fair Ratio of 22.09x. Investors are encouraged to consider these valuation metrics in light of the company's market positioning.

feed.title: Halliburton director Banks sells $88,854 in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Halliburton director Margaret Katherine Banks sold 2,600 shares of common stock for $88,854. This transaction occurred while Halliburton's stock is performing strongly, trading near its 52-week high and showing significant year-to-date gains. The energy services company maintains a $28.75 billion valuation and has a history of consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: PepsiCo Leans on Pricing Power: Is Volume Decline a Growing Risk?
feed.summary: PepsiCo has increasingly relied on pricing to drive revenue growth, offsetting rising input costs and protecting margins, but this strategy has raised concerns about potential volume decline, especially in North America. Other beverage companies like Coca-Cola and Keurig Dr Pepper are also using pricing to protect revenues and margins. PepsiCo is working to balance pricing power with affordability through various initiatives to mitigate the risk of falling volumes and maintain market share.

feed.title: IVZ Stock Gains on Q4 Earnings Beat as AUM, Revenues Increase Y/Y
feed.summary: Invesco (IVZ) reported Q4 2025 adjusted earnings of 62 cents per share, surpassing analyst estimates, with shares gaining 4% in pre-market trading. The company's results were bolstered by increased adjusted revenues and record assets under management (AUM) of $2.17 trillion, despite higher operating expenses and a significant non-cash intangible impairment charge. Full-year adjusted earnings also rose, reflecting strength in its diverse product offerings and balance sheet.

feed.title: BD Biosciences & Diagnostic Solutions Spin-Off to Combine With Waters in Reverse Morris Trust
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has announced that its Biosciences & Diagnostic Solutions business will be spun off to shareholders and subsequently combined with Waters Corporation in a Reverse Morris Trust transaction. The record date for the spin-off is February 5, 2026, and the combination is expected to close on February 9, 2026. BD will receive $4 billion in cash, and BD shareholders will own 39.2% of the combined company, with existing Waters shareholders owning 60.8%.

feed.title: Amazon tipped to top fourth quarter estimates as online spending remains stable
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc is expected to surpass Wall Street's consensus for its fourth-quarter revenue and profit, according to Bank of America analysts. They project higher Q4 revenue and operating income, along with accelerated growth in Amazon Web Services (AWS). Stable online spending, strong advertising trends, and lower fuel costs are cited as key drivers for this anticipated outperformance.

feed.title: More Bill Relief in 2026: PG&E Increases Bill Credit for REACH Program, Puts $50 Million Toward Helping Customers Catch Up on Late Energy Bills
feed.summary: PG&E is committing $50 million in 2026 to help customers pay overdue energy bills through its REACH and Match My Payment programs, which could provide eligible customers with up to $1,800 in combined bill credits. The company is increasing the maximum REACH bill credit from $300 to $800 and also offers the Match My Payment program which matches eligible payments dollar-for-dollar up to $1,000. This initiative follows a 5% cut in residential electric rates and reinforces PG&E's commitment to stabilizing energy costs and assisting customers in need.

feed.title: NexGen spends C$25M to keep 30% stake in IsoEnergy after share sale
feed.summary: IsoEnergy (ISOU) completed a C$25 million non-brokered private placement with NexGen Energy, issuing 1,666,667 common shares at C$15.00 each. This transaction allowed NexGen to maintain its approximately 30% pro rata ownership in IsoEnergy following a separate bought deal financing. The proceeds are intended for the development and exploration of IsoEnergy's mineral properties and general corporate purposes, with the shares subject to a four-month and one-day statutory hold period.

feed.title: Struggling with power bills? PG&E offers up to $1,800 in 2026 aid
feed.summary: PG&E is committing $50 million in 2026 to help customers with overdue energy bills through its expanded REACH and Match My Payment programs. Eligible customers can receive up to an $800 bill credit from REACH and a dollar-for-dollar match up to $1,000 from Match My Payment, totaling up to $1,800 in aid. This initiative follows a 5% reduction in residential electric rates effective January 1, 2026, making rates 11% lower than in January 2024.

feed.title: More Bill Relief in 2026: PG&E Increases Bill Credit for REACH Program, Puts $50 Million Toward Helping Customers Catch Up on Late Energy Bills
feed.summary: PG&E is committing $50 million in 2026 to help customers pay overdue energy bills through its REACH and Match My Payment programs. Eligible customers can receive up to $1,800 in combined bill credits, with the REACH program credit increasing from $300 to $800. This initiative follows the company's fourth electric rate decrease in two years, aiming to stabilize energy costs and provide significant financial assistance to eligible households.

feed.title: Empty Cambridge office building triggers $72M write-off for Arkansas-based Bank OZK
feed.summary: Bank OZK, an Arkansas-based commercial real estate lender, has taken a $72 million write-off on its loan for the vacant 40 Thorndike building in East Cambridge. Despite this hit, the bank remains a significant lender in the Boston area, with nearly $1.4 billion in active loans across various high-profile projects.

feed.title: More Bill Relief in 2026: PG&E Increases Bill Credit for REACH Program, Puts $50 Million Toward Helping Customers Catch Up on Late Energy Bills – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E) has announced a $50 million commitment in 2026 to help customers with overdue energy bills through its REACH and Match My Payment programs. The REACH program's bill credit has increased from $300 to $800, and customers can receive up to $1,800 in combined benefits through both programs. This commitment follows a 5% reduction in residential electric rates effective January 1, 2026, marking the fourth electric rate decrease in two years.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Announces Common Stock Dividend
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company announced that its board of directors has approved a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.45 per share. This dividend is payable on March 1, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 6, 2026. Wells Fargo is a leading financial services company with approximately $2.1 trillion in assets, offering a diversified set of banking, investment, and mortgage products.

feed.title: SLM CORPORATION (NASDAQ: SLM) SHAREHOLDER ALERT Bernstein
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has issued a shareholder alert for SLM Corporation (NASDAQ: SLM) investors, reminding them of an upcoming deadline for a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that SLM Corporation made misrepresentations about its loan mitigation programs and the stability of its private education loan delinquency rates between July 25, 2025, and August 14, 2025. Investors who purchased shares during this period and suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm by February 17, 2026, to discuss their legal options or potentially serve as lead plaintiff.
